reasoning: The combined prediction market probabilities (e.g., Kalshi and Polymarket) and betting odds for the top four favourites—Spain, France, England, and Argentina—total approximately 52%. All of these are past winners. Adding the estimated probabilities of the remaining eligible past winners—Brazil (~10%), Germany (~7%), and Uruguay (~3%)—brings the total probability of a past winner claiming the title to around 72-76%. (Italy has been eliminated in the playoffs and is no longer a contender.) Therefore, the implied probability of a country that has never won the World Cup (e.g., Portugal, Netherlands, Belgium, Colombia) winning the 2026 tournament is approximately 25%. Although historically new winners are rare (only 8 unique winners in 22 editions), a ~24% chance is well-aligned with the historical frequency of a new champion emerging roughly every 4-6 tournaments. The Metaculus community prediction of 0.15 appears slightly too low and does not fully account for the combined probability mass of strong non-winner contenders like Portugal and the Netherlands, nor Italy's recent elimination. I predict 0.24.
